message:
fcp-mesh/gossip: migrate IbltPlaceholder to production IBLT sketch

Addresses flywheel_connectors-kzrfp. The type remains named
`IbltPlaceholder` for ABI continuity (it is re-exported from the crate
root and referenced from the fuzz target), but the implementation is
now a thin wrapper over the production `Iblt` in `crate::iblt` — not
a JSON-serialized `VecDeque<(ObjectId, Option<u32>)>` change log.

## Why the old implementation was wrong

The prior struct stored up to `max_changes` recent object-id / esi
pairs in a VecDeque, serialized the whole deque as JSON over the wire,
and called that "reconciliation". In practice that meant:

- Any divergence larger than `reconciliation_batch_size` silently
  dropped the oldest changes — peers stopped converging on the long
  tail, and the sketch could not reconcile deletions at all.
- Wire overhead was O(N recent changes), not O(d difference) as an
  IBLT is supposed to deliver. The NORMATIVE doc comment claiming
  "precise set reconciliation" was aspirational, not implemented.
- Wire format was `serde_json` rather than canonical CBOR, diverging
  from the FCP3 spec's IBLT serialization.

## What the new implementation does

- `IbltPlaceholder` now owns an `Iblt` sized for the configured
  expected-difference budget, plus a `cell_count` used to reject
  oversized peer sketches. `change_seq` is retained (unchanged) so
  existing metrics observers that sample it keep working.
- `note_local_change` inserts admitted object IDs into the real IBLT
  and increments `change_seq`. The `_esi` parameter is accepted but no
  longer stored — symbol-level ESIs already flow through the separate
  `symbol_filter` / `symbol_availability` path, and mixing them into
  the same change log was confusing and not IBLT-compatible.
- `encode` serializes via `ciborium::into_writer` as canonical CBOR;
  `decode_with_limits` rehydrates via `ciborium::from_reader` and now
  caps cell count (an IBLT-meaningful bound) rather than change count
  (meaningless for a real sketch).
- `recent_changes()` still exists for ABI continuity but always
  returns an empty `Vec`. Callers that used it for reconciliation
  should read from the sketch decode path via
  `Iblt::reconcile_with_peer_iblt`, which `GossipState` already wires
  up at gossip.rs:563.
- `as_iblt() -> &Iblt` accessor added for callers that need direct
  sketch access without going through the wire-format round-trip.
- `MIN_IBLT_BYTES_BUDGET` bumped from 512 to 8192. The prior 512-byte
  floor predates real IBLT serialization and would have rejected all
  but the tiniest real sketches.

## Error taxonomy

`IbltDecodeError`'s `Malformed { detail }` variant now carries CBOR
parse detail (instead of JSON). `TooLarge` and cell-count bounds are
unchanged in spelling but are now measured against real sketch
geometry rather than change-log length.

## Test coverage

- `iblt_change_seq_increments_regardless_of_budget` replaces the prior
  `iblt_bounds_changes` — budget no longer bounds the change log, but
  `change_seq` must keep counting for metrics parity.
- `iblt_encode_is_cbor_iblt_roundtrip` replaces
  `iblt_encode_empty_is_json_array`.
- `iblt_decode_rejects_oversized_cell_count` replaces
  `iblt_decode_rejects_change_limit_exceeded`.
- New `iblt_reconciliation_recovers_full_divergence` exercises a real
  peer-reconciliation round bigger than the old `max_changes` window.
  Under the old placeholder this would have dropped changes; under the
  real sketch, peeling decode recovers the full symmetric difference.

## Non-changes / follow-ups

- `IbltPlaceholder` is kept as the public type name so the fuzz target
  `fuzz/fuzz_targets/mesh_post_verify_summary.rs` and the baseline doc
  in `docs/FCP3_Pre_Cutover_Baseline.md` do not need to rename.
  Renaming the type cleanly is a separate non-blocking task.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
